$comment: Reusable completeness scoring definition. Producers compute a weighted checklist score against a jurisdiction and estate status combination. Consumers can display progress, flag gaps, and compare readiness across estates.
title: Completeness
description: A completeness score derived from a weighted checklist of estate data categories, scoped to a specific jurisdiction and estate status.

score.description: Overall completeness percentage, calculated from the weighted checklist items.

maxScore.description: Maximum possible score for this jurisdiction and estate status combination. May be less than 100 if certain categories are not applicable.

jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code or ISO 3166-2 subdivision code identifying which jurisdiction this scoring applies to.

estateStatus.description: The estate status this completeness score was calculated against. Different statuses may require different data, affecting which checklist items are applicable.

calculatedAt.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this completeness score was computed.

checklist.description: The individual items assessed to produce the overall score. Each item belongs to a category, has a relative weight, and a status indicating whether the data is present.

category.$comment: personal_details: testator identity, addresses, marital status. assets_and_valuations: real property, financial accounts, personal possessions, and their valuations. liabilities: mortgages, loans, debts, guarantees. beneficiaries: named beneficiaries and their shares or bequests. executors: appointed executors and their contact details. guardians: guardians for minor children. tax_information: tax residency, reference numbers, prior returns. legal_documents: wills, codicils, trusts, powers of attorney. lifetime_transfers: gifts, settlements, and transfers within the lookback period. pension_and_insurance: pension schemes, life insurance policies, death-in-service benefits.
category.description: The data category this checklist item belongs to.

item.description: A human-readable label describing what was checked.

weight.description: Relative importance of this item. Higher weights contribute more to the overall score.

status.$comment: complete: the required data is present and valid. incomplete: the data is missing or insufficient. not_applicable: this item does not apply to the current jurisdiction or estate status. unknown: the system cannot determine whether the data is present.
status.description: Whether the data for this item is present and sufficient.

details.description: Optional explanation of the status, such as what is missing or why the item is not applicable.
examples: No valuation on file for 3 of 5 properties
Guardians not required — no minor children
